hint: This grey torpedo has a white belly and rows of serrated triangular teeth it never stops replacing.
funFact: It can detect a single drop of blood in 100 litres of water and senses the tiny electric fields of hiding prey.

hint: Bright orange with three white bands, it shelters unharmed among the stinging tentacles of a sea anemone.
funFact: Every clownfish is born male, and the largest of a group can switch to female when the resident female dies.

hint: A sleek grey mammal with a permanent smile and a curved dorsal fin, famous for leaping and clicking.
funFact: Each dolphin invents a unique signature whistle and uses it like a name to call out to friends.

hint: A gentle reptile with a heart-shaped shell and flippers, it grazes seagrass meadows in warm seas.
funFact: It is named for the greenish fat under its shell, coloured by the seagrass and algae it eats.

hint: A giant diamond-shaped glider with wing-like fins and two horn-shaped flaps beside its mouth.
funFact: With a wingspan up to seven metres, it has the largest brain of any fish and seems to recognise itself in mirrors.

hint: A huge dark whale with knobbly bumps on its head and enormous wing-like flippers that sings long songs.
funFact: Males sing complex songs that can last for hours, and whole populations gradually update the same tune.

hint: A tiny upright fish with a horse-shaped head and a curling tail that grips onto seagrass.
funFact: The males get pregnant, carrying the eggs in a belly pouch until they give birth to tiny fry.

hint: A striking black-and-white hunter with a tall dorsal fin, also known as the killer whale.
funFact: It is actually the largest member of the dolphin family, and different pods speak in distinct dialects.

hint: The largest fish in the sea, a gentle giant covered in a unique pattern of white spots and stripes.
funFact: Despite being longer than a bus, it feeds almost entirely on tiny plankton by filtering seawater.

hint: A slow, big-eyed fish that gulps water to balloon into a spiky sphere when threatened.
funFact: Its body carries a poison more potent than cyanide, yet it is served as the risky delicacy fugu.

hint: A snake-like fish that peers from reef crevices, opening and closing its jaws full of needle teeth.
funFact: It has a second set of hidden jaws in its throat that shoot forward to drag prey down, just like the movie Alien.

hint: A vivid royal-blue reef fish with a yellow tail and a bold black palette-shaped marking on its side.
funFact: It hides razor-sharp spines near its tail and can play dead, lying on its side until danger passes.

hint: A relative of the seahorse draped in leaf-shaped flaps that make it look exactly like drifting seaweed.
funFact: Its leafy limbs are pure camouflage and do no swimming; it drifts using tiny near-invisible fins.
